An unauthenticated remote attacker can exploit an integer overflow vulnerability in the remote message decompression process. By sending a specially crafted compressed remote message before authentication, an attacker can cause memory corruption, leading to a denial of service (DoS) in the CIB remote listener. This can result in the affected service crashing.", "title": "CVE description"}], "product_status": {"recommended": ["SUSE Linux Enterprise High Availability Extension 12
